title: Primed for Action
summary: A quick coding challenge solved — filter primes out of a list and multiply them to get the key.

title: Connected Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
lockedMessage: This machine is currently active on HackTheBox. The full writeup will be published once it retires.
summary: Connected is a Linux machine compromised via CVE-2025-57819, an unauthenticated SQL injection in FreePBX 16 Endpoint Manager, leveraged to plant a cron-triggered web shell for RCE as the asterisk user, followed by privilege escalation through a writable config file executed by a root-owned Incron trigger.

structuredData.headline: Connected Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Connected is a Linux machine compromised via CVE-2025-57819, an unauthenticated SQL injection in FreePBX 16 Endpoint Manager, leveraged to plant a cron-triggered web shell for RCE as the asterisk user, followed by privilege escalation through a writable config file executed by a root-owned Incron trigger.

title: DevHub Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
lockedMessage: This machine is currently active on HackTheBox. The full writeup will be published once it retires.
summary: DevHub is a Linux machine compromised via CVE-2026-23744, an unauthenticated RCE in MCPJam v1.4.2, followed by lateral movement through an exposed Jupyter Lab instance with a plaintext token, and privilege escalation via a hardcoded API key in an internal Flask service running as root.

structuredData.headline: DevHub Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: DevHub is a Linux machine compromised via CVE-2026-23744, an unauthenticated RCE in MCPJam v1.4.2, followed by lateral movement through an exposed Jupyter Lab instance with a plaintext token, and privilege escalation via a hardcoded API key in an internal Flask service running as root.

title: Blackpearl Machine (TCM Security) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux machine via DNS reconnaissance to discover a virtual host running Navigate CMS v2.8, exploited through an unauthenticated RCE vulnerability, followed by privilege escalation via a SUID misconfiguration on php7.3.

structuredData.headline: Blackpearl Machine (TCM Security) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux machine via DNS reconnaissance to discover a virtual host running Navigate CMS v2.8, exploited through an unauthenticated RCE vulnerability, followed by privilege escalation via a SUID misconfiguration on php7.3.

title: Butler Machine (TCM Security) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Windows machine via Jenkins default credentials and Groovy script RCE, followed by privilege escalation through an unquoted service path vulnerability in the WiseBootAssistant service.

toc.value: Remote Code Execution via Jenkins (Groovy Script)

structuredData.headline: Butler Machine (TCM Security) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Windows machine via Jenkins default credentials and Groovy script RCE, followed by privilege escalation through an unquoted service path vulnerability in the WiseBootAssistant service.

title: Kioptrix Level 1 (VulnHub) — Gaining Root with Metasploit
summary: Compromise of a VulnHub Linux machine by enumerating an exposed Samba 2.2.1a service, identifying the trans2open buffer overflow vulnerability, and exploiting it via Metasploit to obtain a root shell directly.

structuredData.headline: Kioptrix Level 1 (VulnHub) — Gaining Root with Metasploit
structuredData.description: Compromise of a VulnHub Linux machine by enumerating an exposed Samba 2.2.1a service, identifying the trans2open buffer overflow vulnerability, and exploiting it via Metasploit to obtain a root shell directly.

title: Soulmate
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine via CVE-2025-31161 authentication bypass on CrushFTP to gain admin access, followed by PHP reverse shell upload, credential extraction from a hardcoded Erlang script, and root flag retrieval through an exposed Erlang shell on port 2222.

toc.value: I tried connecting to port 2222 using SSH with:

structuredData.headline: Soulmate
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine via CVE-2025-31161 authentication bypass on CrushFTP to gain admin access, followed by PHP reverse shell upload, credential extraction from a hardcoded Erlang script, and root flag retrieval through an exposed Erlang shell on port 2222.

title: Knowledge Check Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine running GetSimple CMS, exploited through credential discovery in exposed data files, admin panel access, and a PHP reverse shell upload, followed by privilege escalation via a sudo misconfiguration on php binary.

structuredData.headline: Knowledge Check Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine running GetSimple CMS, exploited through credential discovery in exposed data files, admin panel access, and a PHP reverse shell upload, followed by privilege escalation via a sudo misconfiguration on php binary.

title: Nibbles Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine running Nibbleblog 4.0.3, exploited through default credentials and an authenticated file upload vulnerability to gain a reverse shell, followed by privilege escalation by abusing a world-writable sudo script.

structuredData.headline: Nibbles Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine running Nibbleblog 4.0.3, exploited through default credentials and an authenticated file upload vulnerability to gain a reverse shell, followed by privilege escalation by abusing a world-writable sudo script.

title: Code Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by escaping a Python code editor sandbox via subclass traversal to obtain a reverse shell, extracting and cracking MD5 credentials from a SQLite database, then escalating privileges by abusing a sudo-permitted backup script with a crafted JSON payload to read the root flag.

structuredData.headline: Code Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by escaping a Python code editor sandbox via subclass traversal to obtain a reverse shell, extracting and cracking MD5 credentials from a SQLite database, then escalating privileges by abusing a sudo-permitted backup script with a crafted JSON payload to read the root flag.

title: Artificial Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by uploading a malicious TensorFlow HDF5 model to exploit remote code execution, extracting and cracking MD5 credentials from a SQLite database, then escalating privileges by recovering a Bcrypt-hashed password from a Backrest backup archive and abusing the Backrest web UI to read the root flag.

toc.value: Docker Installation commands for kali linux:
Uploading the profit_model.h5 file to the website :

structuredData.headline: Artificial Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by uploading a malicious TensorFlow HDF5 model to exploit remote code execution, extracting and cracking MD5 credentials from a SQLite database, then escalating privileges by recovering a Bcrypt-hashed password from a Backrest backup archive and abusing the Backrest web UI to read the root flag.

title: Dog Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by dumping an exposed Git repository to extract database credentials, logging into Backdrop CMS as admin, exploiting an authenticated unrestricted file upload vulnerability (CVE-2022-42092) for RCE, and escalating privileges via a sudo misconfiguration on the bee CLI tool.

structuredData.headline: Dog Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Linux HackTheBox machine by dumping an exposed Git repository to extract database credentials, logging into Backdrop CMS as admin, exploiting an authenticated unrestricted file upload vulnerability (CVE-2022-42092) for RCE, and escalating privileges via a sudo misconfiguration on the bee CLI tool.

title: Fluffy Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
summary: Compromise of a Windows HackTheBox Active Directory machine by exploiting CVE-2025-24071 via a malicious library-ms file to capture NTLM hashes via Responder, abusing GenericWrite permissions through BloodHound enumeration to perform Shadow Credential attacks, and escalating to Domain Admin by exploiting an ESC16 ADCS misconfiguration with Certipy.

toc.value: CVE-2025–24071 — NTLM Hash Leak via .library-ms File

structuredData.headline: Fluffy Machine (HackTheBox) — Step-by-Step Walkthrough
structuredData.description: Compromise of a Windows HackTheBox Active Directory machine by exploiting CVE-2025-24071 via a malicious library-ms file to capture NTLM hashes via Responder, abusing GenericWrite permissions through BloodHound enumeration to perform Shadow Credential attacks, and escalating to Domain Admin by exploiting an ESC16 ADCS misconfiguration with Certipy.
